title: Does Rei II know her Identity
display_title: Does Rei II know her Identity
wikitext:
[]: Does Rei II know her Identity?             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Nov 06, 2006 2:36 pm It seems like she possibly knows she's a clone (ie: "The me inside the Eva?") but does she know of who/what? Rei III seems to, though ("I'm probably the Third.", "I'm home.") And because Rei has the soul of Lilith, does that mean in terms of Yui-sama, she only has Yui's physical appearance? Or does she have her DNA, too? If that's the case, yet another reason I'm a S/A fan. An odepius thing for your friend that's to some extent your mom is kinda... Yeah, the above pic says it best. ;

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 06, 2006 2:58 pm Rei is a clone of Yui -- yes. That implies "same DNA"... but this is a show that describes four-eyed and indigo-blooded, or eyeless whale-headed, derivations of Adam "clones", so whatever. Yui is, somehow or other, the genetic template for Rei; that's all you really need to know. Rei can't be a clone in the true sense, since her strange pigmentation appears to be genetically determined (shows up in all of the clones), but that's a quibble, really.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Nov 06, 2006 5:27 pm I believe "replica" would be a better description for the oddball Evas, Rei, and Kaworu. http://dictionary.reference.com/browse/replica

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 06, 2006 6:41 pm The term that is normally used is "copy" (the English word). The CI mentions something about "clone-regeneration/resurrection" (whatever the hell that means) with regard to the relationship between Adam and the Evas. Now that I think about it, I can't recall any official sources that describe Rei as a "clone" (that word) offhand. But it doesn't really matter that much. I remember "copy" being used in a similarly peculiar way in "Final Fantasy VII", wherein "Sephiroth copies" (not "clones", as per the evil Sony translation) have essentially nothing in common with Sephiroth aside from also carrying Jenova cells inside their bodies.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Nov 06, 2006 9:20 pm So...does Rei-chan (ni) know she has Lilith's soul and or that she's a clone of Yui? As I said before, I get the feeling she DOES know she's a clone of something... Square's translation of FF7 sucked ass. When that special Reminesnce of Final Fantasy (AC feature) does a better translation job then Sony/Square did for the acutal game, you know it sucks. JENOVA: "Beacause Cloud, you are a puppet." ME, reading text: WTF?? This WAS translated by "professionals", right? (My POV with FF7 pairings: Cloud/Aries are cute, but I perfer Cloud/Tifa. Or at least Cloud/Aries before you know what then Cloud/Tifa for the rest of the game, etc. XD)

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Nov 06, 2006 9:29 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: So...does Rei-chan (ni) know she has Lilith's soul and or that she's a clone of Yui? As I said before, I get the feeling she DOES know she's a clone of something...

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Nov 06, 2006 9:36 pm Well she knows she's not quite normal: Rei: Who's there? Me? The me inside the Eva . No, I can sense that there's someone other than me. Who are you? An Angel? The person that we call an Angel?

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Mon Nov 06, 2006 10:16 pm That passage always* spoke to me as foreshadowing Human Instrumentality... *after i saw EoE

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Mon Nov 06, 2006 11:04 pm Epsiode 11 "...nor am I the favorite...I know that all too well" She certainly knows shes a clone, and that theres a bunch of her... She may or may not know about the whole yui thing...of course id imagine one would get a little curious as to who you are cloned from... what i wanna know is...was she treated normal at first...and then one day they told her da truth or was she told from the beginning... same question with Kaworu

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Nov 06, 2006 11:12 pm Kaworu was clearly in touch with Adam. His behavior is very motherly. Rei I seemed to be from what we're given in EoTV. Rei II was affraid of Lilith as was Rei III at first: Rei 1: No, you are a person whose false soul was created by the human named Gendo Ikari. You're nothing but an object living a lie, pretending to be a person. See? There's a dark part of your heart inside you that you can see nothing of, that you know nothing of. That's where the real you is. Rei 2: I am me. Because I became who I am through the time I've spent and the connections I've made with other people. Rei 3 (OFF): The current me has been molded through contact with other people. Rei 2 (OFF): Contact with people and the passage of time change the shape of my heart. TEXT: "Is that what ties are?" Rei 2: Yes, the things that have created the me called Rei Ayanami so far... The things that will continue to shape me... TEXT: "That is what ties are" Rei 1: But the real you is somewhere else. You just don't know it. Because you don't want to see it. You're just avoiding it without realizing it. TEXT: "Because you're afraid" Rei 1 (OFF): Because it may not be human in form. Because the person that I have been so far may cease to exist. TEXT: "You are afraid" Rei 1: You're afraid of ceasing to exist. You're afraid of being erased from everyone's hearts. Rei 3: Afraid? I don't understand. Rei 1: Your own individual world will also cease to exist. TEXT: "It frightens you, doesn't it?" Rei 1: You will cease to exist. TEXT: "It frightens you, doesn't it?" Rei 3: No, it makes me happy. Because I want to die. What I desire is despair. I want to return to nothingness. Rei 2: But I can't. I cannot return to nothingness. He won't let me return. Rei 3: He won't let me go back yet. Rei 2 (OFF): I existed because he needed me. Rei 3: But it's over now. He's not going to need me anymore. He's going to abandon me. I thought I had wished for that day to come, but... Now, I am afraid.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 06, 2006 11:23 pm Souls contain an unbelievable amount of information, memories and all. Yui, an individual whose soul was salvaged from one body to another, managed to do so apparently unscathed. (I.e., she didn't lose anything metabiologically.) The transition from Rei 2 to Rei 3, on the other hand, implies the loss of information from one vessel to the next -- and Rei 2 herself was rather incomplete to begin with. Why? Was Rei 1 doomed to an amnesiac condition, as well? Is Kaworu more in-touch with his true identity as Adam simply because he was educated about it, or because Adam's soul was left more intact than Lilith's soul in Rei? Does either soul (have the potential to) exist in a complete state, possessing all of its contents from billions of years (and perhaps even bodies prior to the Seed-vessels of Adam and Lilith)?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Nov 06, 2006 11:38 pm I actually had a theory it stems from her soul being ripped in two by EVA-00. Kyoko went nutty after EVA-02 did the same to her, so it stands to reason Rei wouldn't fare much better.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 06, 2006 11:55 pm "Lilith's soul split between EVA-00 and Rei 2" doesn't really work, does it? How would the two parts ever be recombined? How would it account for the degeneration between Rei 2 and Rei 3? Or how Rei becomes so different once she returns to Lilith? The jist I get, anyway, is that most of the metabiological 'data' has been sitting around in Lilith, even though the 'main unit' was ripped out somehow.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Mon Nov 06, 2006 11:58 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: I actually had a theory it stems from her soul being ripped in two by EVA-00. Kyoko went nutty after EVA-02 did the same to her, so it stands to reason Rei wouldn't fare much better.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Nov 07, 2006 12:02 am Ouch, Reichu you made my head hurt literally. It looked like Kyoko got her soul put back together from her lines in the lake. If your theory is right then I guess the same principal would apply in that instance aswell. Damn you Anno WHY? WHY must it make so little sense?

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Tue Nov 07, 2006 12:08 am Rei III said something along the lines of "This is the first time I've seen these, and yet I feel it is not the first time" in 23. I think that if they combined Rei I and Rei II through their metaphysical magaic they pull every now and again, it probably would cause confusion issues. The Rei II part would recognize so much, while the Rei I part would have missed out on a lot of that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 07, 2006 12:10 am cat42 wrote: I wonder how they catch souls?

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Tue Nov 07, 2006 12:20 am I'd think not, because the souls are firmly stuck into each other? Maybe Anno really does have an answer, but it's just incredibly lame. That's why we are to figurew these things out.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 07, 2006 12:28 am At a public appearance at some nameless anime convention, somebody asked Anno about an irrelevant detail in the popular anime "Evangelion". The question is not particularly important, but Anno's response has left a big impression. "I don't fucking know. Go fanwank something."

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Nov 07, 2006 1:14 am What the fuck? Can Lilith make any less sense? Rei I says Rei's true self is in a dark part inside her heart and yet she says it's somewhere else. Damn you Anno sucking people in with these mysteries!
